hooks.command: echo [SESSION START] && if exist E:\Python\Projekty\Kalahari\.claude\session-state.json (echo Session state found. Consider running /load-session to restore context.) else (echo No saved session found.)
echo === SESSION END === && echo Remember to use /save-session before leaving! && echo Session ended at: %date% %time%
type E:\Python\Projekty\Kalahari\.claude\context\project-brief.txt 2>nul || echo Kalahari | C++20 + Qt6 | Build: scripts/build_windows.bat Debug

hooks.prompt:
Before Claude stops, verify the work is complete:

1. Were any C++ files (.cpp/.h) modified in this session?
2. If yes - was a build attempted after the last modification?
3. If build was not attempted - this is a problem.
4. Are there any unfinished tasks or incomplete implementations?

If C++ files were modified but NO build was run after last change, return:
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"decision": "block", "reason": "Build not verified after code changes. Run: scripts/build_windows.bat Debug"}}

If work appears complete or no code was modified, return:
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"decision": "approve"}}

A task is being marked as completed. Verify quality gates:

1. Was a build run successfully in this session?
2. Were tests run if code was changed?
3. Does the task description mention any acceptance criteria that haven't been met?

If there are clear signs that build/tests were NOT run after code changes, return:
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"decision": "block", "reason": "Quality gate: build/tests not verified. Run build and tests before closing task."}}

If quality appears acceptable, return:
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"decision": "approve"}}

hooks.command: echo {"additionalContext": "[BUILD REMINDER] C++ file modified. Remember to run build before marking task complete: scripts/build_windows.bat Debug"}

hooks.prompt:
Before commit, verify:
1. CHANGELOG.md has entry in [Unreleased]?
2. ROADMAP.md updated if new feature?
3. OpenSpec status current?
4. No TODO in staged files?

Return JSON:
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"permissionDecision": "allow"}} or
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"permissionDecision": "deny", "reason": "..."}}

KALAHARI C++ PATTERNS CHECK:

Check if the code being written/edited violates these patterns:
1. Hardcoded icons: QIcon("path") instead of ArtProvider::getInstance().getIcon()
2. Hardcoded strings: "text" in UI instead of tr("text")
3. Hardcoded colors: QColor(r,g,b) instead of ArtProvider colors
4. Direct qDebug/cout instead of Logger::getInstance()

If ANY violation found, return:
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"permissionDecision": "deny", "reason": "Pattern violation: [describe what's wrong]"}}

If code is OK or you can't determine, return:
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"permissionDecision": "allow"}}
